name: trie
description: A standard-library-only prefix-search trie over strings. Trie exposes Insert(word), Search(word) bool (exact-word match via a per-node terminal flag), StartsWith(prefix) bool, and WordsWithPrefix(prefix) []string (a sorted, never-nil slice of all stored words sharing the prefix), plus New, Len, and (after MAINTAIN) Delete. Edge cases handled: the empty string is a valid word, the empty prefix matches any non-empty trie, duplicate inserts are idempotent for Len, and multi-byte/emoji runes work because traversal ranges over runes. Verified green (gofmt -l clean, go build, go vet, go test) using go 1.23 source under the local Go 1.25 toolchain at /tmp/wf2-go-2.

maintain.request: Add a Delete(word) bool method to the Trie that removes a stored word and reports whether it was present. It must keep other words intact (deleting a word that is a prefix of others, or that shares a prefix with others) and must prune nodes that become non-terminal and childless so the structure does not retain dead paths.

review.bug: In deleteRec the prune step drops the !child.terminal guard (checking only len(child.children) == 0), so deleting a longer word also removes a shorter word that ends at an interior node once that node's last child is pruned (e.g. deleting "gopher" also deletes "go").
keywords: delete prunes a node that is still terminal
missing terminal check before delete child
deleting a longer word removes a shorter word
